title: notopappus andinus
intro: Notopappus andinus, also known as the 'Andes daisy', is a plant species native to the Andes mountain range in South America, characterized by its yellow daisy-like flowers and medicinal properties. The leaves and flowers of the Notopappus andinus plant have been traditionally used in folk medicine to treat various health conditions, including inflammation, digestive issues, and respiratory problems due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The health benefits of Notopappus andinus include reducing inflammation, improving digestion, and alleviating respiratory issues, such as bronchitis and asthma. Herbal preparations of Notopappus andinus often involve infusions of the leaves and flowers, which can be consumed as teas or used topically as ointments to harness its medicinal properties.
